refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency percentile and network conditions to clarify acceptance criteria and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency over the network link under representative conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide timely telemetry and tasking data exchange.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency communication protocols optimized for teleoperation.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to reduce network hops and processing delays.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over other data streams via QoS mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use dedicated low-latency communication protocols optimized for teleoperation.

coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically isolates teleoperation communication protocol, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk of protocol incompatibility with legacy vehicle systems.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP2) if latency targets are not met in testing.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: All software and hardware modules responsible for teleoperation communication and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Incoming vehicle status and sensor data for operator feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control instructions sent to the vehicle.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on teleoperation specifics.
Contains vehicle control but teleoperation communication is external.
Highest containment score; centralizes teleoperation logic and communication.
Focuses on safety alerts, not teleoperation communication.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes teleoperation communication, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact if Teleoperation module interfaces poorly with Vehicle module.
Risk of interface overload on Network Link if not properly managed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface definitions and optimize Network Link usage to reduce latency.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP1 directly addresses the refined FR with clear latency focus and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion if Teleoperation module grows too large or interfaces are not well defined.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for DP1 with minimal diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end via Network Link and Teleoperation module instrumentation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of teleoperation latency responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Define detailed interface protocols for Network Link to ensure latency targets.
Plan integration tests with Vehicle and Safety Services modules to validate end-to-end latency.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and DP integration (e.g., edge computing) is needed to fully mitigate latency risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control system.
FR2: Ensure reliable data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide timely telemetry and tasking data exchange.

revise.why: To consider additional design parameters such as edge computing and QoS prioritization to better guarantee latency under varying network conditions.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can be optimized for latency.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to network variability.
Integration complexity with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data from prototype teleoperation communication protocols.
Interface performance metrics for Network Link under load.
